Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

This will need to be refreshed without "fortestonly" once the test lfsck failures are resolved.  IMHO, we also have the option for adding the sanity-lfsck tests to ALWAYS_EXCEPT to land this patch, but that should not be the first option.

It seems like the reason for these failures is that the netfree change slows down ZFS filesystem operations for some reason?  Have you done any kind of performance comparison with/without this patch to see if other benchmarks are affected?

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 7:

just checked again.

after this:
	local BASE_SPEED1=100
	$START_LAYOUT -r -s $BASE_SPEED1 || error "(2) Fail to start LFSCK!"

obdfilter.*.lfsck_speed_limit=100

after this:
	local BASE_SPEED2=300
	do_facet $SINGLEMDS \
		$LCTL set_param -n mdd.${MDT_DEV}.lfsck_speed_limit $BASE_SPEED2

obdfilter.*.lfsck_speed_limit=100

and only explicit set_param obdfilter.*.lfsck_speed_limit=xxx changes that.

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 7:

> I don't see where the OST speed limit is changed, so it isn't
 > clear how this would affect anything?

Sorry, I was looking at the defaults set in the code, I didn't check the test script.

Patch Set 7:

> Sorry, I was looking at the defaults set in the code, I didn't check the test script.

np, I'm actually not sure what is "right" here.. the initial limit is global, but all the subsequent changes aren't. someone (like me) can find this confusing.
Uploaded patch set 8.

Patch Set 8:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 8:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 8:

Looking at the lfsck_speed_limit behaviour, I think it is reasonable, and in any case it predates this patch and doesn't need to be fixed here.  Specifying a speed limit when LFSCK is started using "lctl lfsck_start -s N" applies to all targets, but specifying it for a specific target with "lctl set_param mdd.*.lfsck_speed_limit=N" only changes the setting (temporarily) for the target that was specified.
Patch Set 8: Code-Review+2

Patch Set 8:

I think the issue is that where is no easy way to change speed limit globally once it's started. not sure how often this is required in practice.

Patch Set 8:

pardon, read "there is no way.."

Patch Set 8:

> I think the issue is that there is no easy way to change speed
 > limit globally once it's started. not sure how often this is
 > required in practice.

That is the same with all global parameters.  Either "conf_param" or "set_param -P" can tune parameters globally, or "pdsh" if that is configured for the servers.

parents.subject: LU-684 tests: replace dev_read_only patch with dm-flakey

commit.subject: LU-8856 osd: mark specific transactions netfree
commit.message:
LU-8856 osd: mark specific transactions netfree

osd-zfs should mark some transactions netfree. this means those
transactions are expected to release space (rather than consume)
and for this kind of transaction half of reserved space is available.

Change-Id: Ia5ca247843b296319376c4ac69efad68b557df9f
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/31444
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Mike Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
